summary: Scorching Shakedown Street jam & epic He’s Gone > Other One
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Shakedown Street > Samson stretch, followed by an inspired He's Gone → The Other One sequence and a tight Black Peter. Jerry's solos are on fire, Brent’s keyboards shine, and the band rides a relentless groove throughout.
review: December 5, 1979 at Chicago’s Uptown Theater captured one of the Winter ’79 high‑water marks. After a brisk first set that included Althea, Big River and a crisp Cold Rain And Snow, the group launched into a blistering second set. Shakedown Street rolls into Samson & Delilah with Brent Mydland weaving melodic counterpoint while Jerry Garcia darts up the fretboard, delivering scorching solos and vocal repeats on Althea. The momentum builds into an eleven‑minute He's Gone that morphs seamlessly into a sprawling Other One jam, highlighted by Phil Lesh’s driving bass “bomblet after bomblet” texture. A tight drums‑to‑space transition leads to a transcendent Black Peter before the band rolls through I Need A Miracle, Bertha and Good Lovin' with unflagging energy. Across all sources the Miller soundboard capture (gd1979-12-05.sbd.miller.112163.flac16) is praised for its crystal‑clear balance, making this performance a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are described with overwhelmingly positive language – ‘scorching’, ‘epic’, ‘best Shakedown ever’, ‘tight drums/space’, and repeated praise for Jerry’s solos, Brent’s keyboards and the overall groove. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard, excellent balance, no dropouts, and captures the full set with every highlighted jam intact.

key_highlights: Monster Shakedown Street > Samson jam that drives the second set.
Epic He's Gone → The Other One sequence with soaring solos and tight bass.
Brent Mydland’s keyboard work shines on Althea, Big River, and throughout the jams.
High‑energy drums/space transition leading into a transcendent Black Peter.

band_performance.Jerry: Runs up and down the frets with scorching solos, vocal repeats on Althea and an extra‑long lead on He’s Gone; his tone is crisp and emotive throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a prominent, driving bass foundation, especially during the He’s Gone → The Other One jam, delivering “bomblet after bomblet” texture.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers strong vocals on several numbers, shines in the end jam of Shakedown Street; rhythm guitar is solid though sometimes buried in mix.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is consistently ‘on the money’, offering warm melodic counterpoint and standout moments on Althea, Big River, Shakedown Street and Ship Of Fools.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming that propels the second‑set momentum; the Drumz segment is highlighted as a choice moment.
